zh.lifeSummary: 當Sibylle Rixton於1280年出生在英國爾時，她的父親Alan de Rixton30歲，母親Petronella De Anyers30歲。她和Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington至少有10個兒子。她去世於1335年在英國英格蘭享年55歲。

nameMeanings.name: Rixton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Angus and Aberdeenshire): habitational name from Roxton Farm in Immingham (Lincolnshire), from the Old English personal name Hrōc (genitive Hrōces) + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Sibylle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alain, Etienne, Jean-Michael.
French: from the female personal name Sibille, from Greek Sibylla (see Sibley ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Sibylle Rixton was born in 1280, in Cheshire, England, her father, Alan de Rixton, was 30 and her mother, Petronella De Anyers, was 30. She had at least 10 sons with Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington. She died in 1335, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55.

Elle a eu au moins 10 fils avec Sir John I Thomas Smith Lord of Carrington. Elle est décédée en 1335, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans.
